title: pelargonium × loudonianum
intro: Pelargonium × loudonianum, also known as Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, derived from the cross between Pelargonium capitatum and Pelargonium odoratissimum. It is valued for its fragrant leaves and flowers, which are used in various herbal preparations due to their medicinal properties, particularly in aromatherapy and traditional medicine. The essential oil extracted from the plant has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms of PMS and menopausal issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Pelargonium × loudonianum are commonly used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
